news.headline: This Strategist Says the Tech Wreck Is Your Buy Signal: Micron and SanDisk on Sale?
news.summary: While the Nasdaq bleeds and investors pile into healthcare and staples at record highs, one Wall Street strategist is shopping the wreckage in memory chips, and her valuation case is harder to dismiss than it looks.

news.headline: Musk and Altman Clash Again
news.summary: The rivalry now spans lawsuits, AI models and competing business plans.

keyLevels.note: Leading the tape at $716.79 — key for risk appetite
Pivot $751.95 — bulls want to hold above

gamePlan: Bias into the open: Bearish — favor defined-risk spreads.
Use QQQ $716.79 as the risk-on/off line; trade with it, not against it.
Primary idea: COIN PUT — enter $8.79–$9.71, target $13.88+, stop $5.55.
Earnings risk: JPM (BMO) — avoid holding short premium through the print.
Keep total options exposure modest; one A+ setup beats five marginal ones.
report.narrative: Futures point to a softer open with QQQ -1.20% and SPY -0.40%. Composite sentiment -33 (Bearish). The tape is being driven mainly by qqq / mega-cap tech, spy trend, sector rotation. VIX at 15.57 (+1.13%). Environment read: favor defined-risk spreads. Energy (+3.10%) is leading while Industrials (-1.88%) lags. Premarket strength in XOM, PLTR; weakness in TSLA, AVGO. The desk's highest-conviction setup is COIN (6.9/10).
report.riskWarning: Options trading is risky and can result in the loss of the entire premium paid. This report provides analysis and educational information only — not financial advice. Size positions small (1–2% of capital per idea), honor stops, and do your own research.
